name: Playback Indicator
overview: Shows direct-play / transcode status badges on episode and movie rows in the Jellyfin web UI.
description: A Jellyfin plugin that automatically calculates whether each episode or movie can direct-play on your current device, and shows a colored badge in the web UI. Badges are calculated using your device's actual playback profile — no manual configuration required.

versions.changelog: Three fixes. (1) Drop the duplicate native tooltip — v0.5.6 set both the title attribute and our own data-jpi-tooltip, so Jellyfin's UI fired the native title tooltip on top of our custom one. Now only data-jpi-tooltip is used; title is replaced with aria-label so screen readers still announce the badge. (2) Tap-to-show on touch devices (Android Mobile, iPhone/iPad Safari) — the custom tooltip used to be hover-only, so it never fired on touchscreens. Now a tap on the badge shows the tooltip for 4 seconds; tap elsewhere to dismiss. (3) Transcode tooltip now leads with what's actually being transcoded — 'video re-encoded, audio re-encoded: ...' or 'video re-encoded, container changed: ...' — so the user can see at a glance whether it's video, audio, both, or container that's incompatible without parsing the detailed reason list.

Two long-standing visual bugs fixed. (1) Icons no longer drop out on Jellyfin Media Player and other Qt WebEngine builds without a color-emoji font. The badge glyphs (✅🔁⚠️❌) were emoji code points; on builds with no emoji font they fell back to monochrome boxes or just disappeared. Replaced all four with inline SVG paths (color via currentColor) that render identically in every webview. (2) Hover tooltips now actually fire everywhere. The native HTML title attribute was unreliable across JMP / themed Jellyfin / Mobile webviews even after v0.5.3 re-enabled pointer events. Replaced with a custom JS tooltip driven by one delegated set of mouseover/mousemove/mouseout listeners on document.body — no longer depends on the browser rendering native title tooltips at all. Native title attribute kept for accessibility / screen-reader fallback.

Tighten native-shell detection so isNativeShell() catches Samsung Tizen and LG webOS via UA / ApiClient._clientName even when window.NativeShell hasn't loaded by the time our boot fires (slower TV browsers). v0.5.4 already routed those clients through the right code path because it always tries (profileBuilder, version) first, but this hardens the synthetic-fallback branch too. Also a README rewrite: 'How It Works' now reflects the actual four-source profile chain (NativeShell → /Sessions → sniffer → synthetic) and the MaxStreamingBitrate sniff; 'A note on Direct Stream' replaced with current Re-mux/Direct Stream split + the browser-MKV-stall caveat; new 'Supported Clients' table makes it explicit which clients can run the plugin (any browser, JMP, Jellyfin Mobile, Tizen, webOS) vs which can't (Swiftfin, native AndroidTV, Roku — no webview).

Fix wrong predictions on Jellyfin Mobile (Android). The Android shell's NativeShell.AppHost.getDeviceProfile(profileBuilder, version) requires a profileBuilder callback to construct the base profile; calling it with no args (as we did for JMP) threw inside profileBuilder({...}), and although the code fell back to /Sessions, that depends on jellyfin-web having posted capabilities first which can race. Now we pass a real profileBuilder that returns a browser-shape synthetic — JMP ignores it, Android uses it and we get its real profile. Also broadened the XHR/fetch sniffer to capture POST /Sessions/Capabilities/Full bodies (the authoritative profile that jellyfin-web posts on connect, fires earlier than any playback call). Synthetic profile builders split into named browser/native-shell variants so the builder we pass into the shell is unambiguous, and both now include empty CodecProfiles/ContainerProfiles/SubtitleProfiles arrays so the Android shell's filter() calls don't crash.

Two fixes. (1) Hover tooltips on the badges now actually fire. v0.5.0's CSS set 'pointer-events: none !important;' on the badge spans so they wouldn't intercept clicks meant for the card — but that also disabled hover, so the native title tooltip never appeared. Re-enabled pointer-events; clicks on the badge still bubble up to the card so navigation isn't broken. (2) Plugin now respects the user's MaxStreamingBitrate setting (Settings → Display → Quality). Was previously hardcoded to 120 Mbps in the prediction call, so items above the user's bitrate cap could falsely show ✅ Direct Play even though the real player would force a transcode. Now sniffed from real player calls (same mechanism as the device profile capture) and persisted to localStorage; result cache invalidates on bitrate change.

Warn when DirectPlay is likely to stall in browsers despite the server / canPlayType saying it should work. Some container+codec combinations (most notably MKV + HEVC, MKV + AV1, AVI) are known-fragile: Chrome reports 'probably' on canPlayType but the actual demux pipeline stalls on real-world 4K HEVC streams. Now the ✅ Direct Play badge shows ' — ⚠ browser MKV demux of HEVC frequently stalls — try Jellyfin Media Player' in the tooltip when one of these combos is detected in a non-native-shell environment. Native shells (JMP, Android) handle these fine and are not flagged. Cache prefix bumped jpi_v7_ → jpi_v8_.

Re-mux / Direct Stream / Transcode tooltips now explain WHY direct play wasn't possible. The server returns TranscodeReasons even when SupportsDirectStream=true (the reasons say which profile criteria the source missed); previously this was only surfaced for full Transcode. Now every non-DirectPlay badge tooltip ends with 'direct play blocked: …' followed by the humanized reason list. Cache prefix bumped jpi_v6_ → jpi_v7_ so existing cached tooltips refresh.

Refactor + cleanup pass. Plugin Configuration (Cache TTL, Show on Movies, Show on Episodes) is now actually honored at runtime — previously the settings were saved but ignored. Cache page works again (was reading stale jpi_v3_ keys when JS writes jpi_v6_) and now includes Re-mux in the badge label and shows the correct deviceId column. Per-item type lookups are batched into one /Items?Ids= request per scan window. Single shared XHR helper replaces 3 copies of XHR boilerplate. Device profile is mirrored into module memory so we don't reparse localStorage every prediction. Cheap profile fingerprint replaces 30KB JSON.stringify churn on profile updates. MutationObserver now ignores changes that don't touch [data-id]. Stale-badge sweep only runs when removals happen. inflight map no longer leaks. URL-poll setInterval dropped in favor of popstate/hashchange listeners. Plugin.cs file IO consolidated into one helper with the same error handling.

Tooltip clarity pass. The ❌ Transcode badge now shows human-readable reasons like 'video codec hevc not supported' / 'HDR variant DOVI not supported' / 'audio channel count 6ch not supported' instead of raw enum names like 'VideoCodecNotSupported, AudioChannelsNotSupported'. Codec / bitrate / resolution / channel values are pulled from the item's MediaStreams. The ⚠️ Direct Stream tooltip now warns that audio re-encode with -c:v copy can stall on long-GOP sources and multichannel audio (full transcode is sometimes faster on a fast CPU — confirmed by Jellyfin issues #6557, #13039, #15430).

Add badge to the item details page (movie / episode) so the user doesn't have to navigate away to see the predicted playback method. Inserted into the item misc-info row next to year / runtime / rating; falls back to the action-button row if the misc row isn't present. Reuses the existing per-item lookup and cache, so opening a detail page for an item that's already been badged in a grid is instant.

Add 🔁 Re-mux badge (blue): items where the container needs repackaging but both video AND audio stay native (lossless, very low CPU). Previously these were lumped under ⚠️ Direct Stream. Splits SupportsDirectStream by checking whether the source audio codec is in the device profile's audio whitelist. Bumped result-cache prefix so old badges get re-evaluated under the new four-way split.
Move overlay badge from top-right to top-left so it no longer covers Jellyfin's played (watched) checkmark indicator on cards.

Fix Jellyfin Media Player / Android still showing everything as Transcode after 0.4.3. Pull the device profile directly from window.NativeShell.AppHost.getDeviceProfile() before falling back to /Sessions — that's the same call jellyfin-web uses to start playback in JMP/Android, so predictions match exactly. Also detect native-shell via ApiClient._clientName as a backup when window.NativeShell loads after our boot.

Fix Direct Stream items mis-marked as Transcode and Jellyfin Media Player / Android Native Shell showing everything as Transcode. Session profile lookup now checks both session.DeviceProfile and session.Capabilities.DeviceProfile (different clients use different paths) and prefers the session whose DeviceId matches ours. Synthetic fallback detects native-shell environments (window.NativeShell or JMP/Android UA) and emits a permissive profile because canPlayType() doesn't reflect mpv/ExoPlayer capability. Console now logs which profile source is in use.

Fix wrong-status-on-everything: pre-fetch the active session's DeviceProfile via GET /Sessions on startup so predictions are accurate from the first scan; sniff fetch() in addition to XMLHttpRequest; broaden the synthetic fallback to include MKV / TS / m2ts containers; invalidate the result cache and re-scan when the captured profile changes; bumped result cache prefix to drop stale predictions from 0.4.0/0.4.1.

Fix badge appearing inside the favorite/overlay button: action buttons that share a card's data-id are now excluded, dedupe prefers the outermost card root, badges placed inside <button> are swept on every scan.

Accuracy + stability rewrite: capture real player device profile, drop polling for MutationObserver, never paint badges on non-playable items, atomic index.html writes, no-store script cache headers, robust uninstall.
